name: Pico del Alar – circular from Sacecorbo through the Alto Tajo Natural Park
name_original: Pico del Alar – circular desde Sacecorbo por el Parque Natural del Alto Tajo
region_intro: Mountain biking around Sacecorbo, located within the Alto Tajo Natural Park, offers diverse terrain characterized by significant geological interest and varied landscapes. The region features a plateau at approximately 1,600 meters in altitude, with rivers carving deep gorges and extensive forests. Mountain bike trails near Sacecorbo Spain utilize a network of tracks and secondary roads, providing options for different skill levels amidst this natural setting. Best mountain bike trails around Sacecorbo The most popular mountain bike trail is Pico del Alar – circular from Sacecorbo through the Alto Tajo Natural Park, an 18.9 miles (30.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park's varied elevations. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Anguita Village and Church – Anguita Village loop from Casa Rural Molino El Salobre, a moderate 27.7 miles (44.6 km) path. This route traverses the landscape around Anguita, offering views of the local villages and natural surroundings. Local mountain bikers also love the Church of Tortonda – Tortonda Municipal Washhouse loop from Cortes de Tajuña, a 20.1 miles (32.3 km) trail leading through rural areas and past local landmarks, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Mountain biking around Sacecorbo is defined by deep river gorges, extensive juniper savin forests, and diverse geological formations. The network of mountain bike trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Sacecorbo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.75 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 14 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sacecorbo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Sacecorbo?
A: Mountain bike trails around Sacecorbo, located within the Alto Tajo Natural Park, feature diverse terrain. You'll encounter a mix of tracks and secondary roads, traversing areas with significant geological interest, deep river gorges, and extensive juniper savin forests. The landscape includes gentle mountains forming a plateau at around 1,600 meters in altitude, offering varied elevations and natural surroundings.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails for all skill levels in Sacecorbo?
A: Yes, the Alto Tajo Natural Park offers a network of trails catering to various skill levels. You can find options ranging from easy paths suitable for beginners to more challenging ascents. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacecorbo – Arrival in Sacecorbo loop from Abánades&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004350521/pico-del-alar-circular-from-sacecorbo-through-the-alto-tajo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Alar – circular from Sacecorbo through the Alto Tajo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is considered difficult.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I discover while mountain biking near Sacecorbo?
A: The region is rich in natural and geological wonders. You can explore areas with diverse rock formations, river gorges, and extensive forests. Notable natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7249951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Waterfall (Cascada de Piedra)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7249954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tormellera Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5445819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Alar&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic views.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, several routes pass by local villages and historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguita Village and Church – Anguita Village loop from Casa Rural Molino El Salobre&lt;/a&gt; offers views of Anguita village and its church. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5445814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Inesperada Salt Factory&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valtablado del Río Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which provides access to a bathing area in the Tagus River.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Sacecorbo?
A: While specific parking areas for each trailhead are not detailed, many routes in the Alto Tajo Natural Park utilize existing tracks and secondary roads, often starting directly from activity centers or villages. It's common to find parking in or near the starting points of routes, such as in Sacecorbo itself or nearby towns like Ocentejo, from where the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31467484" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacecorbo – Arrival in Sacecorbo loop from Ocentejo&lt;/a&gt; begins.

Q: Can I access mountain bike trails in Sacecorbo using public transport?
A: The Alto Tajo Natural Park is a vast area, and while some larger towns might have limited public transport connections, direct access to specific trailheads via public transport can be challenging. It is generally recommended to use personal vehicles or shuttle services, which are sometimes provided by local activity centers, to reach the starting points of mountain bike routes.

Q: Do I need a permit to mountain bike in the Alto Tajo Natural Park?
A: The Alto Tajo Natural Park features over 300 kilometers of well-identified and signposted routes utilizing its network of tracks and secondary roads. General mountain biking on these designated routes typically does not require a specific permit for individual riders. However, if you plan to join guided mountain bike routes or engage in organized events, it's advisable to check with local tour operators or the park authority for any specific requirements.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Sacecorbo?
A: The region experiences extreme temperatures, so spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking. These periods offer milder weather, making for more comfortable riding conditions. Summer can be very hot, and winter can bring cold temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations, which might limit access to some trails.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available near Sacecorbo?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in the Sacecorbo area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004350521/pico-del-alar-circular-from-sacecorbo-through-the-alto-tajo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Alar – circular from Sacecorbo through the Alto Tajo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which starts and finishes in Sacecorbo.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Sacecorbo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.75 stars from over 9 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning geological formations, and the extensive forests that characterize the Alto Tajo Natural Park, providing a unique and engaging riding experience.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that are suitable for families with children?
A: The Alto Tajo Natural Park offers routes that cater to various skill levels, including options suitable for both adults and children. While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, easier routes with less elevation gain and smoother terrain would be more appropriate. It's recommended to check the difficulty grade and distance of individual routes, such as the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacecorbo – Arrival in Sacecorbo loop from Abánades&lt;/a&gt;, to ensure they match your family's abilities.

Q: Are there options for renting mountain bikes or guided tours in the Sacecorbo area?
A: Yes, within the Alto Tajo Natural Park, services such as mid- to high-range bicycle rentals, including helmets and other equipment, are often available. Guided mountain bike routes are also offered, emphasizing areas of high scenic value and significant geomorphological points. These services typically provide logistical support and shuttle services for riders and their bikes.
tips: The Alar Peak is a unique and privileged enclave from where you can see from the top the canyon that the Tagus produces as it passes through the Hundido de Armallones.
Town on the outskirts of the Alto Tajo Natural Park from which very interesting mountain bike routes depart.
